other_observers: Diane Skinner, MaryAnn & Barry Bryant, Liz Brown, Kimberly Johnson; I was original. No other independent observers that I know of.
specific_location: In the Stollsteimer Marsh on SR 151 about 5 miles northeast of the bridge over the Piedre River.
description: The most striking feature of this duck was its uniform olive bill that was slightly lighter at the edges. The head and face was distinctively marked (more than a female Mallard) with a dark, crisp line through the eye and the dark, almost black crown. The face and neck were warm buffy-toned and not very gray. The body overall was fairly dark and warm-toned with slightly rufous-brown coloration. The belly was fairly dark brown. At times the speculum was partially visible and it appeared to be greenish-blue with a very narrow white line on the trailing edge. The tail did not show any white. The legs were a bright orange.Behaviors: This duck was associating with a small group of male and female Mallards in a muddy and marshy area next to open water. It was feeding and walking around in the area. It did not fly while we were observing it.Call: Not heardPlumage: Alternate
